summary: Comprehensive frontend e2e testing of unmark.audio SPA. All critical flows passed: UI loading, hero/dropzone, 3 presets with correct slider values, all 4 toggles, file upload & decoding, processing via OfflineAudioContext, download of cleaned WAV, reset, responsive mobile layout, zero backend API calls, zero console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: audioProcessor.js dithering path connects noise directly to offline.destination even when the main signal chain also connects to destination — dither always plays, which is correct behavior, but note that when all filter stages are disabled, `source` connects to destination only once; OK.
Progress heuristic in processBuffer is a fake UI tick based on elapsed time; this means progress bar may not reflect real render progress — acceptable for MVP.

Minor: Dropzone accepts m4a/aiff but decodeAudioFile relies on browser codecs — Safari vs Firefox may reject certain formats. Not a bug, but a potential UX footgun; consider surfacing a more helpful error message.
File size: all files are well under 700 lines, good separation of concerns.
seed_data_creation: Generated /tmp/test.wav via Python wave module (2s 440Hz mono sine, 44.1kHz, 16-bit PCM, ~176KB) for upload testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is pure static SPA at https://music-no-mark.preview.emergentagent.com. No backend at all. For upload tests, generate a small WAV via Python: `python3 -c "import wave,struct,math; sr=44100; f=wave.open('/tmp/test.wav','wb'); f.setnchannels(1); f.setsampwidth(2); f.setframerate(sr); [f.writeframes(struct.pack('<h',int(32767*0.3*math.sin(2*math.pi*440*i/sr)))) for i in range(sr*2)]; f.close()"`. Key data-testids: dropzone, file-input, selected-file-name, preset-{light,standard,aggressive}, toggle-{lowpass,notches,highshelf,dither}, slider-cutoff, process-button, download-button, reset-button, waveform-original, waveform-processed, audio-original, audio-processed, processing-indicator. All worked. Note: for very short files (<3s), the processing-indicator may not be observable because rendering completes before next tick — not a bug.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Observed that `processing-indicator` was not captured in 800ms post-click for a 2s WAV because OfflineAudioContext rendering finishes faster than the poll interval; the processed waveform appeared within a couple of seconds confirming the pipeline worked end-to-end.
